description: A relay cold-boots with no persisted clock floor and a local clock reading a few seconds after the Unix epoch, so a naive temporal check of the app peer's server certificate against that clock would fail outright (the certificate's real notBefore is decades later). Because the relay's own clock_state is untrusted, it applies skew-tolerant/deferred validation to the app peer's certificate — relying on the trust anchor established at enrollment for identity instead of the certificate's temporal window — completes the handshake, and reaches hello reporting clock_state untrusted; a subsequent clock.hint then adjusts its runtime clock but not its persisted floor.
